source.definition: to take something temporarily with the intention of returning it later

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "borrow" originates from Old English "borgian", meaning to lend or to pledge. It is related to similar words in other Germanic languages and has been used historically to indicate taking something temporarily.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „borrow” provine din engleza veche „borgian”, care înseamnă a împrumuta sau a da în gaj. Este legat de cuvinte similare din alte limbi germanice și a fost folosit istoric pentru a indica luarea temporară a ceva.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used in contexts involving books, tools, or other items.

target.definition: a lua ceva pentru o anumită perioadă, pentru a-l returna mai târziu

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a împrumuta” comes from a Slavic source, probably Bulgarian „prămutja” or a similar Slavic verb meaning "to lend" or "to borrow".
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a împrumuta” provine dintr-o sursă slavă, probabil bulgară „prămutja” sau rusă „приметить”, cu sensul de „a împrumuta” sau „a da cu împrumut”.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation. Used when borrowing something from someone or lending something to someone.

explanation.sourceText: Can also be used in the sense of borrowing an experience.
explanation.targetText: Poate fi folosit și pentru a „împrumuta“ o experiență.

explanation.sourceText: Used to mean giving someone something that must be returned.
explanation.targetText: Pentru a da cuiva ceva temporar, care trebuie returnat.

examples.sourceText: I like to borrow things to my friends.
examples.targetText: Îmi place să împrumut lucruri prietenilor mei.

examples.sourceText: Do you borrow the books from the library?

examples.targetText: Ei împrumută cele mai recente filme.

examples.sourceText: I have borrowed the new novel.

learningTips.text: Remember that "a împrumuta" sounds like "imprumut" which means a loan in Romanian—think of borrowing as taking a loan temporarily.
The verb "a împrumuta" ends with -a, typical for Romanian first conjugation verbs, similar to many English verbs ending in -ate or -ate sounds.
Link the English word "borrow" with "a împrumuta" by the shared idea of taking something temporarily and returning it later.
Think of borrowing a book from a library—both English and Romanian words relate to temporary possession.
